description: FreshnessAI Color-to-pH mapping data. Edit hex codes and HSV values to recalibrate the sensor.

color_stops.label: Highly Fresh / Acidic
color_stops.description: Vibrant red — product is highly acidic and very fresh (e.g., citrus, yogurt)

color_stops.label: Fresh / Mildly Acidic
color_stops.description: Soft pink-mauve — mildly acidic, typical of fresh dairy (e.g., fresh milk, cheese)

color_stops.label: Fresh / Neutral Baseline
color_stops.description: Deep purple-violet — neutral pH baseline, freshly packaged (e.g., water, pure juice)

color_stops.label: Early Spoilage
color_stops.description: Ocean blue-teal — initial bacterial protein breakdown detected

color_stops.label: Advanced Spoilage
color_stops.description: Emerald green — high concentration of ammonia gas from decomposition

color_stops.label: Extreme Decomposition
color_stops.description: Greenish yellow — complete decay, high toxicity, do not consume

dairy.name: Dairy Products
dairy.safety_notes: Dairy products spoil quickly. Discard if pH rises above 8 or if odor is present.

meat.name: Meat & Poultry
meat.safety_notes: Meat is highly perishable. Any sign of spoilage means immediate discard.

fruit.name: Fruits
fruit.safety_notes: Fruits gradually lose acidity as they ripen and decay.

vegetable.name: Vegetables
vegetable.safety_notes: Watch for wilting, sliminess, or off-odors alongside pH changes.

seafood.name: Seafood
seafood.safety_notes: Seafood is extremely perishable. Ammonia smell indicates advanced spoilage.

bakery.name: Bakery & Bread
bakery.safety_notes: Mold growth is the primary spoilage concern for baked goods.

beverage.name: Beverages
beverage.safety_notes: Fermentation or off-flavors indicate spoilage in beverages.
